steps.title: Identify the key temporal signal
steps.content: The passage provides two specific dates: February 14, 1927 (when Vernonia liatroides was collected) and May 3, 1930 (when Achyrocline vauthieriana was collected). These dates reveal a clear temporal relationship between the two collections.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D — Elena Torres collected a sample of Achyrocline vauthieriana in May of 1930, after collecting Vernonia liatroides in February of 1927. This choice uses “after collecting Vernonia liatroides in February of 1927” to show that the 1930 sample came later — the word “after” directly expresses the temporal sequence.

The other three choices focus on location, provide a single date in isolation, or emphasize geography and plant family rather than timing.

That matches Choice D — Elena Torres collected a sample of Achyrocline vauthieriana in May of 1930, after collecting Vernonia liatroides in February of 1927.
